title: Operators
summary: Provides core multilinear operator constructors and their evaluation rules.

mathematicalContent.heading: Mathematical role
mathematicalContent.body: Provides core multilinear operator constructors and their evaluation rules.

mathematicalContent.heading: How to read the translation
mathematicalContent.body: The correspondence below follows PolyGJMSoperators/Operators.lean declaration by declaration. A definition is translated by its defining expression, a theorem by its quantified proposition, and a structure, class, or inductive type by the mathematical data encoded in its fields or constructors. The original Lean signature remains visible beside every translation.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The zero rank-r multi-operator. In explicit terms, for r in the natural numbers, its defining expression is fun maps to zero Conf.
Pointwise sum of rank-r multi-operators. In explicit terms, for r in the natural numbers, and p, q of type cal conf.multi operator r, its defining expression is fun F U maps to P F U plus Q F U.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{multiAdd}(P,Q)=\operatorname{fun} F U => P F U + Q F U
correspondence.leanType: def multiAdd {r : ℕ} (P Q : CalConf.MultiOperator r) : CalConf.MultiOperator r

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Pointwise difference of rank-r multi-operators. In explicit terms, for r in the natural numbers, and p, q of type cal conf.multi operator r, its defining expression is fun F U maps to P F U minus Q F U.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{multiSub}(P,Q)=\operatorname{fun} F U => P F U - Q F U
correspondence.leanType: def multiSub {r : ℕ} (P Q : CalConf.MultiOperator r) : CalConf.MultiOperator r

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Scalar multiple of a rank-r multi-operator. In explicit terms, for r in the natural numbers, c in the real numbers, and p of type cal conf.multi operator r, its defining expression is fun F U maps to c times P F U.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \operatorname{multiSmul}(c,P)=\operatorname{fun} F U => c \cdot P F U
correspondence.leanType: def multiSmul {r : ℕ} (c : ℝ) (P : CalConf.MultiOperator r) : CalConf.MultiOperator r

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Apply a unary operator after a rank-r multi-operator. In explicit terms, for r in the natural numbers, l of type function conf maps to function conf, and p of type cal conf.multi operator r, its defining expression is the function sending F to L P F.
correspondence.leanType: def postcomp {r : ℕ} (L : Function Conf → Function Conf) (P : CalConf.MultiOperator r) : CalConf.MultiOperator r

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Apply a unary operator to one input slot before a rank-r multi-operator. In explicit terms, for r in the natural numbers, i of type fin r, l of type function conf maps to function conf, and p of type cal conf.multi operator r, its defining expression is the function sending F to P Multi Input.replace F i L F i.
correspondence.leanType: def precompSlot {r : ℕ} (i : Fin r) (L : Function Conf → Function Conf) (P : CalConf.MultiOperator r) : CalConf.MultiOperator r

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Apply slot-dependent unary operators before a rank-r multi-operator. In explicit terms, for r in the natural numbers, l of type fin r maps to function conf maps to function conf, and p of type cal conf.multi operator r, its defining expression is the function sending F to P the function sending i to L i F i.
correspondence.leanType: def precompAll {r : ℕ} (L : Fin r → Function Conf → Function Conf) (P : CalConf.MultiOperator r) : CalConf.MultiOperator r

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For r in the natural numbers, and F of type Cal Conf.Multi Input r, Cal Conf.multi Zero r F equals zero Conf.
correspondence.leanType: theorem multiZero_apply {r : ℕ} (F : CalConf.MultiInput r) : CalConf.multiZero r F = zero Conf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For r in the natural numbers, P, Q of type Cal Conf.Multi Operator r, F of type Cal Conf.Multi Input r, and U of type Conf.Ambient Bundle, Cal Conf.multi Add P Q F U equals P F U plus Q F U.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,r\colon \mathbb{N},\;P,Q\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{MultiOperator} r,\;F\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{MultiInput} r,\;U\colon \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{AmbientBundle},\quad \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{multiAdd} P Q F U = P F U + Q F U
correspondence.leanType: theorem multiAdd_apply {r : ℕ} (P Q : CalConf.MultiOperator r) (F : CalConf.MultiInput r) (U : Conf.AmbientBundle) : CalConf.multiAdd P Q F U = P F U + Q F U

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For r in the natural numbers, c in the real numbers, P of type Cal Conf.Multi Operator r, F of type Cal Conf.Multi Input r, and U of type Conf.Ambient Bundle, Cal Conf.multi Smul c P F U equals c times P F U.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,r\colon \mathbb{N},\;c\colon \mathbb{R},\;P\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{MultiOperator} r,\;F\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{MultiInput} r,\;U\colon \operatorname{Conf}.\operatorname{AmbientBundle},\quad \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{multiSmul} c P F U = c \cdot P F U
correspondence.leanType: theorem multiSmul_apply {r : ℕ} (c : ℝ) (P : CalConf.MultiOperator r) (F : CalConf.MultiInput r) (U : Conf.AmbientBundle) : CalConf.multiSmul c P F U = c * P F U

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: For r in the natural numbers, L of type Function Conf maps to Function Conf, P of type Cal Conf.Multi Operator r, and F of type Cal Conf.Multi Input r, Cal Conf.postcomp L P F equals L P F.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: \forall\,r\colon \mathbb{N},\;L\colon \operatorname{Function} \operatorname{Conf} \longrightarrow \operatorname{Function} \operatorname{Conf},\;P\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{MultiOperator} r,\;F\colon \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{MultiInput} r,\quad \operatorname{CalConf}.\operatorname{postcomp} L P F = L (P F)
correspondence.leanType: theorem postcomp_apply {r : ℕ} (L : Function Conf → Function Conf) (P : CalConf.MultiOperator r) (F : CalConf.MultiInput r) : CalConf.postcomp L P F = L (P F)

references.note: Source for multilinear tangentiality and Laplacian-word constructions.

limitations: The displayed Lean results are checked relative to structures, typeclasses, certificates, or model laws supplied as hypotheses. The module does not by itself derive every interface field from a concrete geometric backend.
